data.narrative:
Precision Neuroscience is a Series C-stage biotech company that has raised $180M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 4 same-stage, same-sector private companies in Biotech & Health (Ultima Genomics, Insitro, Colossal Biosciences, and New Limit), with valuations ranging from $810M to $4.0B, plus 3 public comparables in the sector.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $180M total raised as a proxy for current enterprise value and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, yields $306M. Peer-median valuation across the 4 private comparables yields $1.9B, with a range spanning $810M to $4.0B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (median 6.1x across RXRX, TEM, and ONT.L) is not applicable due to absence of target revenue disclosure.

The two applicable methods support a triangulated range of $260M (low) to $2.2B (high), with a median of $1.1B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around product stage maturity, clinical validation progress, and market opportunity size relative to the peer cohort. Stage-progression implies a significantly lower valuation than peer median, suggesting either a below-peer risk profile or earlier clinical/commercial inflection relative to comparables.
